artifacts.summary:
## Overview
The meeting aimed to introduce the project, discuss the project plan, and practice using project tools. The team consists of a Project Manager (Jen), a Marketing Expert (Cat), an Industrial Designer (Gaurav), and a User Interface Designer (Maarika). They are working on creating a new remote control for a television that is original, trendy, user-friendly, and intuitive.

## Key Discussion Points
### Project Plan and Roles
* The team will work individually initially and then come together for meetings to work on the project.
* The team will use a menu-based system or a single button for a menu to simplify the remote control.
* The Project Manager will keep things going and ensure the team works together productively.
* The Industrial Designer will design the details of the product, including its functionality and components.
* The User Interface Designer will be responsible for designing the technical functions, interface concept, and user interface design at different stages of the project.
* The Marketing Expert will focus on the user requirements specification, trend watching, and product evaluation.

### Design Requirements
* The remote control should be original, trendy, user-friendly, and intuitive.
* It should have a high battery life, but not at the expense of usability.
* The team should balance originality with usability to avoid a steep learning curve for new users.
* The design should not be overly complicated, as this can lead to user confusion.

### Production Costs and Profit Targets
* The team aims to sell the remote controls for 25 Euros each on the international market.
* The production costs should be a maximum of 12.5 Euros to reach a profit target of 50 million Euros.

## Decisions Made
* The team will use a menu-based system or a single button for a menu to simplify the remote control.
* The team will aim for a high battery life, but not at the expense of usability.

## Open Questions
* What special features should the remote control have to make it unique and out of the ordinary?
* How can the team balance originality with usability to avoid a steep learning curve for new users?
* What are the production costs and profit targets for the remote control, and how can the team ensure they are met?
artifacts.followups:
## Email
Subject: Recap of First Meeting and Next Steps

Dear Team,

I wanted to follow up on our first meeting and recap the key decisions and action items. As discussed, our project aims to create a new remote control for a television that is original, trendy, user-friendly, and intuitive. We will be working individually initially and then coming together for meetings to work on the project.

We decided to use a menu-based system or a single button for a menu to simplify the remote control. We also discussed the importance of balancing originality with usability to avoid a steep learning curve for new users. Additionally, we agreed to aim for a high battery life without compromising usability.

Before our next meeting, please review the project plan and the technical functions design. For Maarika, please focus on the user requirements specification. We will discuss our findings and progress in our next meeting.

Please bring your laptops and any relevant materials to the next meeting. We will also need to unplug the whiteboard and other equipment.

Looking forward to seeing you all in 30 minutes.

Best regards,
Jen
Project Manager

## Slack
Reminder: Next meeting in 30 minutes. Please review the project plan and technical functions design. Aim for a high battery life without compromising usability. Bring laptops and relevant materials.

- The team will use a menu-based system or a single button for a menu to simplify the remote control.

## Email
Subject: Recap of First Design Project Meeting

Dear Team,

This email serves as a recap of our first design project meeting, which took place on [date]. We discussed the project plan, introduced ourselves, and practiced using project tools. The main decisions and action items from the meeting are as follows:

* We will work individually initially and then come together for meetings to work on the project.
* The team will use a menu-based system or a single button for a menu to simplify the remote control.
* We aim for a high battery life, but not at the expense of usability.
* We will consider unique features that are out of the ordinary, such as a menu-based system or a single button for a menu, to make the remote control original and user-friendly.

Please review the meeting notes and action items, and feel free to reach out to me if you have any questions or concerns.

Best regards,
Jen (Project Manager)

## Slack
Reminder: Project Meeting Recap

We discussed the project plan, introduced ourselves, and practiced using project tools. Key decisions include:

* Working individually initially, then coming together for meetings
* Menu-based system or single button for menu to simplify remote control
* High battery life, but not at expense of usability
* Considering unique features to make remote control original and user-friendly

Next meeting in 30 minutes. Carry laptops back and unplug things.

## Overview
The meeting aimed to introduce the project, discuss the project plan, and practice using project tools. The team created a new remote control for a television that is original, trendy, user-friendly, and intuitive. The team will work individually initially and then come together for meetings to work on the project.

## Key Discussion Points
### Project Overview
* The team will create a new remote control for a television that is original, trendy, user-friendly, and intuitive.
* The team will work individually initially and then come together for meetings to work on the project.

### Project Plan
* The project plan has three components: functional design, conceptual design, and detailed design.
* The team will start with functional design, then conceptual design, and finally detailed design.

### Design Considerations
* The team should balance originality with usability to avoid a steep learning curve for new users.
* The team should not overcomplicate the design, as this can lead to user confusion.
* The team should keep in mind the production costs and profit targets when designing the remote control.

### Design Ideas
* The team discussed the idea of a menu-based system or a single button for a menu to simplify the remote control.
* The team also discussed the idea of a high battery life, but not at the expense of usability.
* The team should aim for a design that is sleek and stylish, but still functional.

### User Interface
* The team discussed the idea of a user-friendly interface that is easy to use.
* The team should consider the user requirements specification, technical functions design, and user interface design.

## Decisions Made
* The team will work individually initially and then come together for meetings to work on the project.
* The team will use a menu-based system or a single button for a menu to simplify the remote control.
* The team will aim for a high battery life, but not at the expense of usability.

## Open Questions
* What special features should the remote control have to make it stand out from existing ones?
* How can the team balance originality with usability to avoid a steep learning curve for new users?
* What are the production costs and profit targets for the remote control?

## Email
Subject: Recap of First Meeting and Next Steps

Dear Team,

This email is a brief recap of our first meeting and the next steps for the project. We discussed the project plan, which includes working individually initially and then coming together for meetings to work on the project. We also touched on the importance of balancing originality with usability to avoid a steep learning curve for new users.

Some key points to keep in mind:

* We aim to create a new remote control for a television that is original, trendy, user-friendly, and intuitive.
* We will work individually initially and then come together for meetings to work on the project.
* We will aim for a high battery life, but not at the expense of usability.
* We will discuss and finalize the project plan in our next meeting.

Please review the meeting notes and action items, and let me know if you have any questions or concerns. I look forward to our next meeting in thirty minutes.

Best regards,
Jen

## Slack
We discussed the project plan and decided to aim for a high battery life without compromising usability. We also touched on the importance of balancing originality with usability to avoid a steep learning curve for new users. Let's keep these points in mind as we move forward with the project.

## Overview
The meeting aimed to introduce the project, discuss the project plan, and practice using project tools. The team consisted of a Project Manager (Jen), Marketing Expert (Cat), Industrial Designer (Gaurav), and User Interface Designer (Maarika). They discussed the project's goals, including creating a new remote control for a television that is original, trendy, user-friendly, and intuitive.

## Key Discussion Points
### Project Plan and Roles
* The team will work individually initially and then come together for meetings to work on the project.
* The team will use a menu-based system or a single button for a menu to simplify the remote control.
* The team will aim for a high battery life, but not at the expense of usability.

### Design Considerations
* The team should balance originality with usability to avoid a steep learning curve for new users.
* The team should not overcomplicate the design, as this can lead to user confusion.
* The team should keep in mind the production costs and profit targets when designing the remote control.
* The team discussed the importance of making the remote control user-friendly and intuitive, with a focus on simplicity and minimal buttons.

### Design Ideas
* The team discussed the idea of a menu-based system or a single button for a menu to simplify the remote control.
* The team considered the idea of a high battery life, but not at the expense of usability.
* The team discussed the importance of making the remote control original, trendy, and user-friendly.

### Production Costs and Profit Targets
* The team discussed the production costs and profit targets for the remote control, with a goal of selling the remote controls for 25 Euros each and aiming for a profit of 50 million Euros.
* The team noted that the production costs should be kept low, with a maximum of 12.5 Euros per remote control, to reach the profit target.

## Decisions Made
* The team will work individually initially and then come together for meetings to work on the project.
* The team will use a menu-based system or a single button for a menu to simplify the remote control.
* The team will aim for a high battery life, but not at the expense of usability.

## Open Questions
* The team did not discuss or decide on specific features for the remote control, but rather brainstormed ideas and concepts.
* The team did not finalize the design or production plan for the remote control.
